title: varasia alsinoides
intro: Varasia alsinoides, also known as Indian pennywort or Varahi, is a perennial herb native to the tropical and subtropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its various health benefits. The plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to support liver and kidney function. Varasia alsinoides can be prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
